title: Sample Workflow with BSBReport
text: One essential bullpen analysis pitch movement plot, identifying various pitch types “profiles”. devleoping good arsenal, understanding movement profile needed change grips, intent, general movement trends. , can use Pitch_Plot. function outputs summary table plot pitch movement data. call, specify PlayerId select one pitcher.  plotted output groups pitch types color shape, allowing clear distinctions. look exact averages, table provides information. data can make practical insights pitch quality consistency. key component understanding pitchers data location execution. Plate_Viz Master_Summary come play.
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(knitr) Pitch_Plot(trackman_data, PlayerId = Demo_Player) #> $plot #>  #> $table #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   TaggedPitchType Pitch_Count Avg_Speed Avg_Vertical_Break Avg_Horizontal_Break #>   <chr>                 <int>     <dbl>              <dbl>                <dbl> #> 1 Changeup                  5      81.7               6.8                 18.3  #> 2 Curveball                 1      77.3              -8.47               -10.2  #> 3 Cutter                    2      84.2              10.4                  0.06 #> 4 Fastball                  1      88.2              15.5                 12.2  #> 5 Sinker                    3      87.3              12.7                 17.1  #> 6 Slider                    6      80.3               1.42                -8.72

title: Sample Workflow with BSBReport
text: two primary functions revolve around location execution side pitch design. First, visualize general trends strike zone misses based pitch type, Plate_Viz works best. Built using standard zone measurements (plate height dimensions), plot table use plate location data trackman map pitches messing . table gives us averages based pitch type , allowing birds eye view pitch location  command Master_Summary can used get full analysis movement location data. “bang buck” function prints html table, rendering higher quality. also contains Strike Percentage calculation based standard strike zone measurements. nasty pitch movement perspective effective can thrown strike zone. get easy sense strike percentage, column color-coded ranked descending order. can see, sinker changeup command great spot, slider accurate pitch session. Together, functions BSBReport allow key analytical insights bullpen quality assessment.
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(knitr) Plate_Viz(trackman_data, PlayerId = Demo_Player) #> $plot #>  #> $table #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   TaggedPitchType Pitch_Count Avg_Speed Avg_Plate_Height Avg_Plate_Side #>   <chr>                 <int>     <dbl>            <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 Changeup                  5      81.7             1.37          -0.46 #> 2 Curveball                 1      77.3             0.52          -1.45 #> 3 Cutter                    2      84.2             2.17          -1.24 #> 4 Fastball                  1      88.2             1.55          -0.78 #> 5 Sinker                    3      87.3             1.69           0.69 #> 6 Slider                    6      80.3             1.38          -0.6 library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(knitr) library(kableExtra)  Master_Summary(trackman_data, PlayerId = Demo_Player)

title: Trackman Data Analysis
text: goal BSBReport assist analyzing trackman data bullpens live bats. BSBReport generates multiple plots tables better understand pitch data future execution pitch design programming. utilizes trackman raw data, contains ball flight metrics pitch captured. Within metrics, package focuses Pitch Velocity, Induced Vertical Break, Horizontal Break, Plate Location. Pitch Velocity: velocity recorded ball leaves pitchers hand Induced Vertical Break: amount movement, inches, applied ball via spin pitcher accounting gravity. Can think applied movement pitcher pitch. Horizontal Break: amount movement, inches, applied ball via spin pitcher horizontal plane. Offspeed pitches usually higher HB values account increased side spin. Plate Location: Location approximate ball crosses home plate. Displayed “Pitch Height” “Pitch Side”, can plotted visualize calculate strike percentage.

title: Trackman Data Analysis
text: can install development version BSBReport GitHub :

title: Trackman Data Analysis
text: BSB Report three main functions view pitch movement data, pitch location data, summary data session. 3 functions use, relating different aspect bullpen data. use BSBReport, standard trackman file must used. looking demo data, can see two distinct PitcherIds, well pitch type key metrics.

title: Trackman Data Analysis
text: Pitch_Plot can used visualize summarize movement data session. output yields color-shape coded movement plot summary table averages relevant metrics.
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(knitr) Pitch_Plot(trackman_data) #> $plot #>  #> $table #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   TaggedPitchType Pitch_Count Avg_Speed Avg_Vertical_Break Avg_Horizontal_Break #>   <chr>                 <int>     <dbl>              <dbl>                <dbl> #> 1 Changeup                  5      81.7               6.8                 18.3  #> 2 Curveball                 5      76.3              -9.39                -9.71 #> 3 Cutter                    3      84.2              10.8                  0.45 #> 4 Fastball                  3      88.3              15.4                 12.9  #> 5 Sinker                    3      87.3              12.7                 17.1  #> 6 Slider                    6      80.3               1.42                -8.72

title: Trackman Data Analysis
text: Plate_Viz examines location pitches relative strike zone plate. Offering table exact location averages along relevant metrics, subsequent plot gives visual pitches landed compared strike zone.
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(knitr) Plate_Viz(trackman_data) #> $plot #>  #> $table #> # A tibble: 6 × 5 #>   TaggedPitchType Pitch_Count Avg_Speed Avg_Plate_Height Avg_Plate_Side #>   <chr>                 <int>     <dbl>            <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 Changeup                  5      81.7             1.37          -0.46 #> 2 Curveball                 5      76.3             1.58          -0.7  #> 3 Cutter                    3      84.2             2.1           -1.12 #> 4 Fastball                  3      88.3             1.82          -0.21 #> 5 Sinker                    3      87.3             1.69           0.69 #> 6 Slider                    6      80.3             1.38          -0.6

code:
library(dplyr) #> #> Attaching package: ‘dplyr’ #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:stats’: #> #> filter, lag #> The following objects are masked from ‘package:base’: #> #> intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(ggplot2) library(knitr) library(kableExtra) #> #> Attaching package: ‘kableExtra’ #> The following object is masked from ‘package:dplyr’: #> #> group_rows #mini sample trackman dataset demo_trackman_data <- data.frame( TaggedPitchType = c("Fastball", "Curveball"), PitcherId = c(1122334455, 2233445566), RelSpeed = c(88.23, 80.35), InducedVertBreak = c(15.25, -6.44), HorzBreak = c(12.87, -3.45), PlateLocHeight = c(1.73, 2.14), PlateLocSide = c(0.84, 0.92)) Master_Summary(demo_trackman_data) #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #> #>

title: Generating Pitch Movement Plot and Summary Table — Pitch_Plot
code: library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(knitr) #mini sample trackman dataset demo_trackman_data <- data.frame(   TaggedPitchType = c("Fastball", "Curveball"),   PitcherId = c(1122334455, 2233445566),   RelSpeed = c(88.23, 80.35),   InducedVertBreak = c(15.25, -6.44),   HorzBreak = c(12.87, -3.45))   Pitch_Plot(demo_trackman_data) #> $plot  #>  #> $table #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   TaggedPitchType Pitch_Count Avg_Speed Avg_Vertical_Break Avg_Horizontal_Break #>   <chr>                 <int>     <dbl>              <dbl>                <dbl> #> 1 Curveball                 1      80.4              -6.44                -3.45 #> 2 Fastball                  1      88.2              15.2                 12.9  #>

code: #mini sample trackman dataset library(dplyr) library(ggplot2) library(knitr) demo_trackman_data <- data.frame(   TaggedPitchType = c("Fastball", "Curveball"),   PitcherId = c(1122334455, 2233445566),   RelSpeed = c(88.23, 80.35),   PlateLocHeight = c(1.73, 2.14),   PlateLocSide = c(0.84, 0.92))   Plate_Viz(demo_trackman_data) #> $plot  #>  #> $table #> # A tibble: 2 × 5 #>   TaggedPitchType Pitch_Count Avg_Speed Avg_Plate_Height Avg_Plate_Side #>   <chr>                 <int>     <dbl>            <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 Curveball                 1      80.4             2.14           0.92 #> 2 Fastball                  1      88.2             1.73           0.84 #>

title: A sample Trackman pitching dataset to highlight the funcitons in BSBReport — trackman_data
A sample Trackman pitching dataset to highlight the funcitons in BSBReport — trackman_data
text: data frame 25 rows 8 columns: PitcherId Integer value denoting unique player id TaggedPitchType Character denoting pitch type PitchCount integer pitch count InducedVertBreak integer representing IVB pitch HorzBreak integer representing HB pitch PlateLocHeight integer measured pitch height front home plate PlateLocSide integer measured pitch horizontal location front home plate RelSpeed integer measured pitch speed release mph
